2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Clienteling.n: ENTRY POINT — Houses' clienteling / VIC programs (Chanel, Dior, D&G, etc.)
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Clienteling.ds: Invitations to couture shows, dinners and trips (e.g. D&G Alta Moda) are handed out by the clienteling teams to Very Important Clients. The path: shop regularly at ONE boutique, be assigned a dedicated sales associate (SA), leave your contact details, ask to be invited to the boutique's events (launches, cocktails, trunk shows) — the invitations rise in prestige with your purchase history.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Clienteling.p: In practice: 1) choose a house and a flagship boutique; 2) request a personalized appointment (personal shopping); 3) sign up for the client files and newsletters; 4) aim first for the local boutique events, then the private presentations; the great houses' official shows remain reserved for press/buyers/VIC.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Clienteling.ci: The pinnacle of the system is the Dolce & Gabbana Alta Moda (12-17 July 2026 in Italy), reserved for couture clients — a documented model of access through purchase.

2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Concierges d.n: ENTRY POINT — Palace concierges (Clefs d'Or): the key to impossible tables
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Concierges d.ds: The Clefs d'Or concierges of the palace hotels (Crillon, Ritz, Byblos, Hôtel de Paris...) secure for their resident guests VIP tickets to prestigious events, unfindable tables (Club 55, Caves du Roy) and sometimes invitations to private events of partner houses. The service is included in the stay.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Concierges d.p: Book a stay (even a short one) at a palace hotel at the right time (Paris 5-9 July for couture; Saint-Tropez/Monaco mid-July for polo and galas) and reach out to the concierge WELL IN ADVANCE by e-mail with precise requests (Red Cross gala 18/7, polo final, Club 55 table).
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Concierges d.ci: The global Les Clefs d'Or network (around 4,000 members).

2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Silencio (cl.n: ENTRY POINT — Silencio (private club, Paris): membership by application
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Silencio (cl.ds: A private club founded by David Lynch, frequented by the fashion and film set and highly active during fashion weeks (afterparties). Verified annual membership: La Carte 900 EUR/year (75 EUR/month), La Carte + 1,680 EUR/year, La Carte - (reduced rate) 600 EUR/year (under 30 or foreign residents). Members admitted from 6pm; online applications reviewed by the club, with sponsored candidates given priority.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Silencio (cl.p: Submit an application at lesilencio.com/apply (the club seeks out creative and artistic communities); a recommendation from a member speeds up acceptance (the sponsor earns one complimentary month). Alternative without membership: tables via platforms such as Clubbable on select evenings.

2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Les lieux-sc.n: ENTRY POINT — Venues open to reservation (Hôtel Costes, Club 55, Les Caves du Roy, VIP Room)
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Les lieux-sc.ds: Much of the luxury "scene" is simply pay-to-enter: the bar/restaurant at the Hôtel Costes (Paris, a fashion headquarters during couture), Club 55 at Pampelonne (the yacht crowd's lunch), Les Caves du Roy at the Byblos (open every evening from 26 June to 29 August 2026 — verified; Fridays and Saturdays from April), the VIP Room. No membership required: a reservation (and a budget) is enough.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Les lieux-sc.p: Book directly: lescavesduroy.com (table/bottle), Club 55 by telephone several days ahead in July, the Hôtel Costes through its website. Going on couture weeknights (Paris, 6-9/7) or polo-final weekends (Saint-Tropez) maximizes encounters with the milieu.

2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Billetteries.n: ENTRY POINT — Official VIP ticketing for luxury sport (polo, motorsport, showjumping)
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Billetteries.ds: VIP hospitality can be bought online with no connections whatsoever: Polo Club Saint-Tropez (grandstands and VIP offers via the site's ticketing, otherwise free entry), Le Mans Classic Legend (VIP & Hospitality packages — the 2-5 July edition now past), Longines Global Champions Tour (tickets.gcglobalchampions.com — the Paris leg confirmed, now past, held 19-21 June 2026 at the Champ-de-Mars, with further European legs in summer).
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Billetteries.p: Buy directly: polo-st-tropez.com (ticketing section), ticket.lemansclassic.com, tickets.gcglobalchampions.com. The VIP areas at these events are the most accessible on the "luxury" calendar: prices displayed, no co-optation required.

2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes aux e.n: ENTRY POINT — Auctions and public viewings by the auctioneers (Christie's, Sotheby's, Drouot)
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes aux e.ds: The pre-sale exhibitions (jewelry, watches, Hermès bags, wine) at Christie's (av. Matignon), Sotheby's (Galerie Charpentier) and Drouot are free and open to all, no appointment needed: there you rub shoulders with collectors and the houses' specialists, and can attend the sales in the room.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes aux e.p: Consult the online calendars (christies.com/en/calendar, sothebys.com, drouot.com) and sign up for the "luxury" newsletters; to bid, create an account (a security deposit may apply). Previews of prestigious sales often accept online registration.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes aux e.ci: A direct gateway to the sales in NYC, London, Geneva and Hong Kong.

2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes press.n: ENTRY POINT — Press sales and private sales at the fashion houses
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes press.ds: "Press sales" (houses' archives and samples at reduced prices) run on lists: boutique client files, specialist newsletters and word of mouth within the milieu. July, the sales month, also concentrates the private sales reserved for cardholding clients, opening a few days before the public sales.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes press.p: 1) Sign up to boutiques' client files (see the clienteling entry) to receive "private sale" invitations ahead of the sales; 2) follow the accounts/newsletters that announce the Parisian press sales; 3) for strictly-run press sales, access comes through a recommendation from an employee or journalist at the house.
2026-07-01|POINT D'ENTRÉE — Ventes press.ci: The same practice as in Milan and NYC (sample sales).

2026-07-04|Vente aux enchères des archiv.ds: A landmark sale of some 200 lots drawn from the personal archives of Martin Margiela (1984-2008): garments, prototypes, photographs, drawings and objects, in an exhibition staged by Bob Verhelst. Result: EUR 1.39 million in total, with a record EUR 364,000 for a pair of graffitied Tabi boots from 1991; Japanese, Korean and Chinese buyers…

2026-07-04|LUMA Arles — Semaine d'ouvert.dt: Opening week 4-7 July 2026; summer exhibitions from 4 July to 8 November 2026 and beyond
2026-07-04|LUMA Arles — Semaine d'ouvert.ds: Maja Hoffmann's foundation opens its summer season with three major exhibitions: 'CORRESPONDENCES' by Soundwalk Collective & Patti Smith at the Grande Halle (4 July-8 November), 'Bodies Never Lie' by Stan Douglas and 'Amanat, The Sacred Forest' by Saodat Ismailova. LUMA also hosts the Dior Photography and Visual Arts Award for young talents, and the PRINT platform (food, music, image) on the east side of the campus.

Sun 5 July: Patti Smith signing 11am-12pm (free, no reservation), PRINT opening 3pm-1am (free), Soundwalk Collective & Patti Smith performance 8:30pm-9:30pm (sold out), DJ set 10pm-1am. Mon 6 July: 2nd Patti Smith performance 8:30pm (sold out), free DJ sets. Tue 7 July: Opening Talks 11am-1pm (free, with Hans Ulrich Obrist, Daniel Birnbaum, Francesco Vezzoli), Stan Douglas screening at the Théâtre Antique 9:45pm (by reservation).

2026-07-06|Paris Haute Couture Week Auto.n: Paris Haute Couture Week Autumn-Winter 2026-2027 (official FHCM calendar)
2026-07-06|Paris Haute Couture Week Auto.ds: Official haute couture week organised by the Fédération de la Haute Couture et de la Mode (FHCM), ~30 houses presenting the Autumn-Winter 2026-2027 collections. An edition marked by the couture debuts of Pierpaolo Piccioli at Balenciaga and Duran Lantink at Jean Paul Gaultier, and the first appearances of Manish Malhotra and Standing Ground.
2026-07-06|Paris Haute Couture Week Auto.sw: Couture week by strict invitation, a global elite from Cardi B to Deneuve, exceptional venues.
2026-07-06|Paris Haute Couture Week Auto.p: Shows by house invitation only (couture clients, press, buyers, celebrities). For non-professionals: 1) free livestreams on the FHCM/parisfashionweek website and the houses' channels (Dior, Chanel, Schiaparelli, Elie Saab, Armani Privé broadcast live or on replay), 2) paid public Stéphane Rolland show at the Olympia, 3) Dior installation at the Musée Rodin open to the public 7-12 July, 4) standing outside the venues (Petit Palais, Grand Palais, Musée Rodin) to see celebrity arrivals, 5) becoming a couture client of a house or going through a palace concierge.
2026-07-06|Paris Haute Couture Week Auto.pe: Bad Bunny, Michelle Yeoh, Emma Corrin, Tilda Swinton, Catherine Deneuve, Cardi B, Demi Moore; Jonathan Anderson (Dior), Matthieu Blazy (Chanel), Pierpaolo Piccioli (Balenciaga), Duran Lantink (Jean Paul Gaultier), Manish Malhotra (Bollywood debut)

TUESDAY 7/07: Chanel two showings (10:00 AM and 12:00 PM), Stéphane Rolland 2:30 PM (Olympia, public), Giorgio Armani Privé two showings 6:00 PM and 7:00 PM (also Alexis Mabille, RVDK, Germanier, Ashi Studio). WEDNESDAY 8/07: Balenciaga 11:30 AM, Elie Saab 2:30 PM, Jean Paul Gaultier late afternoon, Manish Malhotra 8:00 PM (also Yuima Nakazato, Franck Sorbier, Robert Wun, Viktor&Rolf, Zuhair Murad). THURSDAY 9/07: Adeline André 4:00 PM (also Celia Kritharioti, Peet Dullaert, Rami Al Ali, Aelis); Sidaction Fashion Dinner from 7…

2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.ds: August is the fallow month of the runway calendar: the top models are on holiday or making paid appearances across the Mediterranean. Verified 2026 source fact: Bella Hadid photographed aboard a yacht in Saint-Tropez in late May 2026 after Cannes (hola.com, TMZ, W Magazine). Note: the photos of Kendall Jenner on a yacht in Ibiza date from 2024, not 2026 (claim corrected). Documented spots: Club 55 and the beaches of Pampelonne, Nikki Beach, the beach clubs of Ibiza, Mykonos, Cos…
2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.sw: World-renowned top models (Bella Hadid confirmed) aboard yachts and on private beaches, yet an informal, non-ticketed circuit.
2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.p: The venues are commercially accessible: table reservations at Club 55, Loulou, Nikki Beach, the beach clubs of Pampelonne (book several weeks in advance in August, preferably through a hotel concierge). The brands' private beach soirées remain by invitation/PR only.
2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.pe: Bella Hadid (Saint-Tropez, May 2026, confirmed); Naomi Campbell and Kate Moss, Riviera regulars according to the local press (not dated 2026)

2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.iv_o: No organiser: this is an editorial/paparazzi phenomenon (models on holiday), not a single event. The venues involved are independent commercial establishments — Club 55, Loulou, Nikki Beach, the Pampelonne beach clubs, and their counterparts in Ibiza, Mykonos and on the Costa Smeralda.
2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.iv_g: Each establishment handles its own reservations (telephone, email, SevenRooms); the private brand parties that draw the models (house dinners, cruise events) are run by the maisons' PR/press offices, by invitation only.
2026-08-01|Circuit vacances des mannequi.iv_w: There is no single event to «access»: you go where the models go in summer. Realistic route: book a table or a sunbed at the documented spots (Club 55 — by telephone only, Loulou, Nikki Beach, the Pampelonne beach clubs) several weeks ahead, ideally through the concierge of a palace hotel, who secures the best placements. The private brand parties (house dinners, cruise shows) where the models appear remain invitation/PR only: getting close presupposes a link with a maison or its agency. There is no «models» contact to approach — only the venues can be reached.

2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.n: Concerts at the Prince's Palace 2026 — Martin Rajna & Zoltán Fejérvári (OPMC)
2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.ds: A concert by the Monte-Carlo Philharmonic Orchestra in the Court of Honour of the Prince's Palace, a tradition created by Rainier III and Princess Grace more than 60 years ago. Conducted by Martin Rajna (principal conductor of the Hungarian State Opera), with pianist Zoltán Fejérvári: Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 3 in C minor, Op. 37 and Dvořák's Symphony No. 8 in G major, Op. 88.
2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.sw: A ticketed concert, yet held in the Court of Honour of the Prince's Palace, residence of Prince Albert II.
2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.p: Online ticketing at opmc.mc or montecarloticket.com (until 2h before the concert); OPMC box office at the Atrium of the Casino de Monte-Carlo, Place du Casino, tel. +377 92 00 13 70. 2026 prices: Carré d'Or 175 €, then from 120 € to 20 € (Cat. 5); group (10+) and youth (under 25) rates. Formal city attire required, jacket and tie compulsory for gentlemen. Tickets still available as of verification (July 2026).
2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.pe: Martin Rajna (conductor), Zoltán Fejérvári (piano); concerts given at the invitation of H.S.H. Prince Albert II in his residence

2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.iv_g: The OPMC box office handles the seats (counter at the Atrium of the Casino de Monte-Carlo + online sales on opmc.mc / montecarloticket.com); the OPMC administration (Auditorium Rainier III) handles press requests — no dedicated external press office
2026-08-02|Concerts au Palais Princier 2.iv_w: Box office open to the public — this is not an invitation-only event. 2026 prices: Carré d'or €175, then from €120 down to €20 (Cat. 5), with group (10+) and youth (under 25) rates; tickets still available at the time of checking (July 2026). Purchase online on opmc.mc or montecarloticket.com, or at the Atrium counter in the Casino. Formal city dress required, jacket and tie mandatory for men. The Cour d'Honneur has limited capacity: book early. Free press route: accreditation request to the OPMC administration (info@opmc.mc, +377 93 10 85 00).

2026-08-14|Fête de la Libération de Sain.ds: Commemoration of the Provence landings (15 August 1944): the liveliest day of the summer, with the grand fireworks display fired over the gulf the evening before — yachts take up position in the bay from early evening.

2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.n: Receptions and buyers' cocktail parties around the Arqana sales
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.ds: A documented tradition of the August sales: champagne reception on opening night, consignors' cocktail parties and buyers' dinners at Deauville's finest tables. The 2026 programme of these receptions has not been published online.
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.sw: Invitation-only receptions for the racing elite: champagne, buyers' dinners, a very closed circle.
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.p: By invitation from Arqana, the consignor stud farms or bloodstock agents. Registered buyers and their guests are invited; go through your agent, or contact Arqana (info@arqana.com) or your hotel concierge (Barrière) for the private dinners.
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.pe: Owners, breeders and agents from the international circuit
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.ci: Flexjet, for instance, organised buyers' stays in 2023 with a champagne reception on opening night and hosted dinners throughout the sale; a recurring tradition but unconfirmed for 2026
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.iv_o: Arqana (thoroughbred auction house, Deauville, chaired by Olivier Delloye); receptions co-hosted with the consignor stud farms on their stands
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.iv_g: No external PR agency documented: communications handled in-house by Arqana (Agathe Capdepon, head of communications); each consignor stud farm runs its own buyers' cocktail parties
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.iv_w: The realistic route: register as a buyer — the 'application for approval' (bank references) is sent to compta@arqana.com; once approved, you gain access to the ring, the consignors' yards and the buyers' receptions. A simpler alternative: appoint a bloodstock agent or accredited broker, who registers their client and brings them along to the stud farms' cocktail parties (Écurie des Monceaux, etc.). The sales are public; the cocktail parties are earned through the consignor relationship, not through paid tables (no prices published).…
2026-08-14|Réceptions et cocktails achet.sej_pitch: Between glasses of champagne at the Arqana sales and strolls along Les Planches, experience Deauville in a palace hotel: Norman art de vivre on the edge of the turf.

2026-07-01|The Championships, Wimbledon.dt: 29 June – 12 July 2026 (second week and finals within the window)
2026-07-01|The Championships, Wimbledon.ds: The absolute society fixture of the London Season. The Centre Court Royal Box (by personal invitation of the Chairman of the All England Club, strict dress code: jacket and tie) gathers royals, stars and the international elite. Debentures (the only legally resaleable tickets, on the same level as the Royal Box) grant access to private restaurants and bars. Premium hospitality. Finals over the weekend of 11–12 July.

2026-07-01|Henley Royal Regatta 2026.dt: 30 June – 5 July 2026 (6 days, Tuesday to Sunday)
2026-07-01|Henley Royal Regatta 2026.ds: The most elegant sporting event of the English summer. The Stewards' Enclosure (the holy of holies) is reserved for members and their guests — no ticket can be bought — with a strictly enforced dress code (turned away without a jacket, without a tie/cravat, or in jeans or shorts). The Regatta Enclosure, by contrast, is open to ticket holders. Champagne, Pimm's and rowing blazers: the quintessence of British chic.

2026-07-01|Henley Royal Regatta 2026.p: Be invited by a member for the Stewards' Enclosure; otherwise buy a Regatta Enclosure ticket at hrr.co.uk. VIP hospitality packages (Above+Beyond, Sincura).

2026-07-01|Henley Royal Regatta 2026.ht: Racing during the day; enclosures open throughout the day

2026-09-02|Mostra de Venise 2026 — 83e F.iv_w: Two avenues. 2026-09-28|Fashion Week Paris — invitati.iv_w: Two paths. PRESS: obtain the free FHCM accreditation (accreditation@fhcm.paris), then write to the press office of the house you are targeting to request the show invitation — the contacts above are the published professional channels. VIC CLIENT (the route without being press): become a client of a house, stay loyal to one same sales advisor in the boutique and express the wish to be invited; a significant purchase history qualifies you for VIC status, which opens show invitations and the front row. No seat at the show is "bought": it is earned through the relationship. Free in both cases — it is the relationship (press or client) that grants access.

2026-09-28|Accès payant — billetterie de.iv_w: A fully paid route, no press or client status required. AFFORDABLE: Fashion Week Online sells seats to independent designers' shows (~€150 per show, up to VIP tiers). MID-RANGE: PassToLocal offers a 'designer show' package from €1,480; The Bureau Fashion Week and Flying Solo ticket independent shows. HIGH-END: concierges AC Private (3-to-8-day packages from €8,000 to €20,000) and Sincura (on quote) craft bespoke access and itineraries. Important: these shows are independent / 'off-calendar', distinct from the official shows of the major houses (which are not sold).

2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.ds: Set on a rocky cove of Île Sainte-Marguerite, reachable only by boat, La Guérite transforms at dusk into a dinner facing the lights of Cannes. 2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_pitch: A stay set to the rhythm of the Mediterranean, between the Belle Époque splendour of the Croisette and the wild serenity of the Îles de Lérins. After sunset and a dinner at the water's edge at La Guérite, the Mediterranean spell lingers on — a seafront palace hotel, a starred table in the hills and a crossing to the monks' vineyards of Saint-Honorat. Cannes' art of living at its rarest: the sea for a backdrop, luxury for a tempo.
2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_hotels0: A five-star palace hotel facing the Croisette and the Palais des Festivals: private beach, heated pool and Spa by Biologique Recherche. The perfect haven for returning from the island at first light, just minutes from the boarding pontoon.
2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_hotels1: An Art Deco icon of the Croisette, 410 sea-facing rooms and suites, a private beach and the vast garden-spa L'Oasis by Carita. The refinement of the Roaring Twenties, a step from Quai Laubeuf where the boats set off for Sainte-Marguerite.
2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_tables0: A Michelin-starred gastronomic table, Mediterranean cuisine by Jean Imbert in a cinema-inspired setting, with panoramic views over the bay. An exceptional dinner for the evening that stays off the island.
2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_tables1: Two Michelin stars and Relais & Châteaux, in an 18th-century Provençal bastide in the hills above Cannes. Bruno Oger's haute cuisine, between Breton terroir and the Riviera, around a patio shaded by centuries-old trees.
2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_exp0: Daily departures from Quai Laubeuf in Cannes to Sainte-Marguerite and Saint-Honorat (15-minute crossing), or a sunset coastal cruise along the Estérel. The most elegant way to reach the archipelago and prolong the magic of the Mediterranean.
2026-07-17|La Guérite – dîners au couche.sej_exp1: On the neighbouring island of Saint-Honorat, Cistercian monks tend 8 hectares of vines. Cellar and tasting of rare cuvées (Clairette, Chardonnay, Viognier, Syrah, Mourvèdre, Pinot Noir) and of the Lérina liqueur — a spiritual, sensory interlude between two evenings.

2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_pitch: Set down your bags before the emerald waters of Romazzino and extend the magic of the Robbie Williams gala well beyond the 12:50 AM DJ set, without ever taking to the road. Wake up on the private beach, a signature Shiseido spa ritual, lunch with your feet in the sand then a Michelin-starred dinner on the old harbour of Porto Cervo: these few days turn an exceptional evening into a complete Sardinian art of living. A private escape by sea toward the coves of La Maddalena seals this turnkey stay, amid pink granite, fragrant maquis and the turquoise Mediterranean.
2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_hotels0: The very palace of the gala: sleep where the evening unfolds. An icon designed by Michele Busiri Vici (1965), reopened after renovation, private sandy beach, four hectares of gardens, white architecture facing the emerald sea. The ultimate choice to extend the night without moving an inch.
2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_hotels1: The founding grand palace of the Costa Smeralda (1963), minutes from Romazzino, on one of the island's most beautiful bays. 121 frescoed rooms and suites, an Olympic pool facing the open sea, a Shiseido Spa and legendary five-star service.
2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_tables0: The gourmet table of Porto Cervo, on the Promenade du Port facing the Old Harbour. 2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_tables1: The iconic waterside restaurant of the Vecchio Molo, the very first restaurant built on the Costa Smeralda (1960s, Jacques Couelle). 2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_exp0: A private day with a skipper departing from the marina of Porto Cervo toward the national park of La Maddalena: translucent coves, secret anchorages and southern Corsica within a sail's reach. Sailing yacht, catamaran or RIB, tailor-made.
2026-08-09|BIG ART Festival — Robbie Wil.sej_exp1: A Japanese wellness ritual drawing on 140 years of expertise and the spirit of Omotenashi: signature face and body treatments, dedicated hammams, Spa Journeys scented with Kuroho. The perfect recovery the morning after the gala.

2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.n: ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Opéra Royal Gala Dinner
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.ds: ADOR's 9th charity gala dinner in aid of the Opéra Royal: champagne reception (Salle des Croisades), gala concert 'From Salzburg to Naples: lyric jewels of Mozart, Rossini, Donizetti', cocktails in the Salon d'Hercule, a musical interlude in the Chapelle Royale and a promenade through the Grands Appartements, grand supper in the Galerie des Batailles, then fireworks from the Galerie des Glaces. A patronage dinner of the highest order, with individual seats or tables of 10, open to donors (patronage tax deduction).
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.sw: Patronage in its most exalted form: champagne in the Salle des Croisades, bel canto under the baton of Gaétan Jarry, a grand supper in the Galerie des Batailles and fireworks set off from the Galerie des Glaces — Versailles returned to the night for a chosen few.
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.ci: The 9th annual Gala of the Amis de l'Opéra Royal (ADOR), a pillar of cultural patronage at the Château de Versailles and the season's premier society occasion.
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.pe: Gwendoline Blondeel (soprano) and soloists of the Académie de l'Opéra Royal; Choir and Orchestra of the Opéra Royal under the baton of Gaétan Jarry (Mozart / Rossini / Donizetti programme)
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.iv_o: ADOR — Les Amis de l'Opéra Royal de Versailles
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.iv_w: Bienfaiteur seat €1,400 or Grand Bienfaiteur €1,600; Grand Bienfaiteur package with a night at the Waldorf Astoria Trianon Palace Versailles €3,700 (for two); Prestige table €16,000 or Premium €20,000 (10 seats). Patronage tax deduction. Online booking closed: contact the ADOR office (official phone/email) or the box office on 01 30 83 78 89 (Mon–Fri 11:00–18:00), 3 bis rue des Réservoirs, 78000 Versailles.
2026-10-03|ADOR's Gala Dinner 2026 — Dîn.sej_pitch: Extend the magic of the Opéra Royal by staying just steps from the Château's gates. Between a palace nestled within the very Estate of Versailles and the Belle Époque elegance of the Trianon Palace, your stay embraces the French art of living: a Michelin-starred dinner, a Guerlain spa and an early-morning boat outing on the Grand Canal. 2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.dt: Season from 21 June to 1 September 2026, Wednesday–Sunday (00:00–06:00)
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.ds: A closed-door club on the peninsula (Elegance Hospitality group), with meticulous stage design and an international crowd, open from midnight until dawn. Sharp house/techno programming with world-class DJs all summer long. Way in: online ticketing (Shotgun/InList) and table reservation.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sw: Ramatuelle's after-dark club: world-class DJs from midnight to dawn, the Saint-Tropez night at its sharpest.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.ci: Ramatuelle's new jet-set nightlife benchmark; dress code, valet, selective door
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.pe: Richy Ahmed (03.07), Andrea Oliva (08.07), Agoria (10.07), LP Giobbi (22.07), Jimi Jules (06.08) — InList summer 2026 line-up
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.iv_w: Online ticketing (Shotgun/InList); table reservation by telephone
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_pitch: Sanctum opens its nights from 00:00 to 06:00, Wednesday to Sunday, from 21 June to 1 September 2026, on chemin des Matarane: the tipping point of a day that begins far earlier. The stay is set on the ridges of Ramatuelle, minutes from the club, among umbrella pines and garrigue with a due-south view over the bay — late rising, spa, lunch with your feet in the sand at Pampelonne, a cru classé rosé cellar in the late afternoon, a starred table at nightfall. The club is not the outing of the stay, it is its final act: you arrive after dinner, on foot or by chauffeured car, and you are back in bed ten minutes later.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_hotels0: Five stars, 736 chemin des Crêtes in Ramatuelle, on the heights facing the bay: 8 rooms, 19 suites with terrace and 14 private villas with garden and pool. An 800 m² Spa Nescens devoted to wellbeing, fitness and beauty treatments. The closest base to Sanctum, a few minutes' drive away, and the only address of the stay that also houses its own two-starred table.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_hotels1: A five-star palace perched above Saint-Tropez, at the heart of a 13-hectare park of umbrella pines and cypresses, with the bay of Pampelonne on the horizon. Turrets, domes and arcades, rooms and suites, five restaurants and a 1000 m² Valmont spa (adults-only indoor pool, nine treatment rooms, hammam, sauna, fitness). It joined the Airelles collection in 2019 and reopened after a complete renovation in 2021.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_tables0: Two stars in the Guide MICHELIN 2026 and the closest fine-dining table to the club. Chef Eric Canino signs a Mediterranean cuisine of great lightness, built around fish, olive oil and the fruit, vegetables and herbs from the house kitchen garden. Served in the evening beneath the white sails of the terrace, facing the Mediterranean. 736 chemin des Crêtes, Ramatuelle — booking essential, including for non-residents.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_tables1: Three MICHELIN stars and 19/20 in the Gault&Millau for Arnaud Donckele, trained under Michel Guérard and Alain Ducasse, three-starred since 2013. An obsessive reading of Mediterranean fish and of the sauces that have become his signature. Dinner only, from 19:30 to 21:30, closed on Wednesday — precisely the night off that precedes the Sanctum nights. About fifteen minutes from Ramatuelle.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_exp0: The Pampelonne institution since 1955, 43 boulevard Patch in Ramatuelle, a few hundred metres from the club. Lunch from 12:15 until around 16:00, sun loungers on the sand, the house philosophy unchanged. 2026 season: Friday 27 March to Sunday 1 November. Table reservation by telephone only (+33 4 94 55 55 55), to be arranged some fifteen days ahead in high season.
2026-06-21|Sanctum Saint-Tropez — saison.sej_exp1: A Côtes de Provence cru classé since 1955, 110 hectares of vines in the Gassin valley facing the gulf of Saint-Tropez, and one of the last estates in Provence still harvesting by hand. Guided tour of the cellar and vineyard lasting about an hour and a half, followed by a tasting, by reservation. Open Monday to Friday from 9:00 to 19:00, Saturday and Sunday from 10:00 to 13:00 and from 15:00 to 18:00. Ten minutes from Ramatuelle: the rosé you will find again that same evening at your table in the club.

2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.dt: Summer season 2026 (July-August); recurring venue. Line-up and headliner dates to be confirmed on the official Line-Up page and the events calendar; the 2026 headliners have not been announced to date.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.ds: Pampelonne beach club (Yeeels group) that turns into an electro stage as the day fades. To be checked as the 2026 dates are announced.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sw: The Byblos beach at Pampelonne after dark: exceptional DJ sets barefoot in the sand, the jet set on the terrace.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.ci: Jet-set table on Pampelonne, champagne showers and star DJ sets; yacht clientele
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.pe: HISTORIC headliners (not confirmed for 2026): David Guetta, Calvin Harris, Martin Garrix, Black Coffee, Marco Carola, CamelPhat
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.iv_g: Table or VIP booth with minimum spend, or lunch on the beach
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.iv_w: Table booking by telephone or official email; consult the Line-Up page or the website calendar for artist dates
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_pitch: Three days in the light of the peninsula: waking up facing the bay from the heights of Ramatuelle, barefoot lunch on the sand at Pampelonne, a siesta in the shade of pines and vines. Once night falls, Verde Beach picks up the thread of the beach — music, salt and open sky — a few cabanas from your lunch table. In between: the cellar of a Cru Classé de Provence, a three-starred table and a spa to set the body back to summer time.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_hotels0: Five-star hotel perched on the garrigue of Ramatuelle, facing the bay: rooms, suites and private villas, a vast spa, the starred restaurant La Voile and a private beach on Pampelonne. The most natural base from which to radiate out to Verde Beach, a few minutes away by car.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_hotels1: Palace hotel set in a 19th-century château, at the heart of a 12-hectare park overlooking the gulf, at the gateway to Saint-Tropez. Ceremonial suites, Valmont spa, several restaurants and a concierge service cut out for Saint-Tropez nights.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_tables0: The peninsula's three-starred table, under the direction of Arnaud Donckele: signature sauces, jus and infusions, terrace opening onto the bay of Bouillabaisse. A gala dinner to be booked very far in advance for high season.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_tables1: The Pampelonne institution since 1955, a few cabanas from Verde Beach: lunch barefoot in the sand, vegetables and olive oil from Domaine des Bouis, daytime service only (approx. 10:00-17:00). Booking essential in July and August.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_exp0: A Cru Classé de Provence ten minutes from Pampelonne: guided tour of the cellars and the vines followed by a tasting in the cellar, open all season. The rosé of the peninsula at the source, before the evening service.
2026-07-01|Verde Beach Saint-Tropez — nu.sej_exp1: A sanctuary of marble and precious woods at the heart of the Byblos, with hammam, sauna and water area ahead of the treatments, signature rituals and massages developed with Sisley. The ideal recovery morning after a night on the beach (day pass / spa pass available).

2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.n: Shellona St-Tropez — 2026 music season (Pampelonne beach club)
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.dt: Summer 2026 season (late May–late September), DJ programming according to the club calendar. PLEASE NOTE: the dated headline nights (Black Coffee 16 July & 4 Aug., Solomun 18 July, Rüfüs Du Sol 26 July, Peggy Gou 28 July, Diplo 12 Aug., Damian Lazarus 17 Aug.) are announced by concierge sources (AC Private) and NOT confirmed by the official Shellona website — dates to be verified with the club.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.ds: A Greek-chic beach club on Pampelonne that turns into an electronic stage from the afternoon onwards. According to concierges (AC Private), the 2026 season should line up a roster of world-class DJs (Solomun, Black Coffee, Peggy Gou, Rüfüs Du Sol, Diplo...), yet the official website publishes no such dated line-up. Champagne-lunch mood, then dancing at sunset. Access: lunch with no minimum spend (150-300 € per person) or a table with minimum spend on event nights.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sw: Pampelonne's Greek-chic beach in music mode: live sets and DJs at sunset, tables in fierce demand.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.ci: One of Pampelonne's 'see and be seen' spots; arrivals by yacht or helicopter from Monaco, Cannes and Nice; superstar dates sold out several weeks ahead
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.pe: Solomun, Black Coffee (Grammy), Peggy Gou, Rüfüs Du Sol, Diplo, Damian Lazarus, Ben Böhmer, Jamie Jones, Joseph Capriati, Adriatique
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.iv_g: Lunch with no minimum spend; evening tables with minimum spend (approx. 1,000–5,000 € depending on the artist, concierge source)
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.iv_w: Table reservation via SevenRooms (official link from shellona.com), or official phone/email
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_pitch: Three suspended days on the peninsula: umbrella pines, terraced vines and that late-afternoon light that tips Pampelonne from idleness into celebration. Base yourself in Ramatuelle or on the heights of Saint-Tropez, days spent between golden sand, a cru classé cellar and a three-star table, evening at Shellona, chemin des Tamaris, when the decks take over from the sun. Concierge note: the 2026 DJ programming varies with the club's calendar, and the announced headline nights (Black Coffee, Solomun, Rüfüs Du Sol, Peggy Gou, Diplo, Damian Lazarus) come from third-party sources not confirmed by Shellona — we have the date and the table confirmed directly with the club before any booking.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_hotels0: A confidential palace hotel set in the garrigue above the bay of Pampelonne: 12 villas and suites designed by Rémi Tessier, Nescens spa with indoor pool and hammam, Michelin-starred restaurant La Voile by chef Éric Canino. A few minutes from the beaches and from the village of Saint-Tropez — the most discreet address for coming home late without meeting a soul.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_hotels1: A 19th-century château with turrets and arcades, 12 hectares of cypress and jasmine overlooking the beaches of Pampelonne and the vineyards of Ramatuelle. 86 rooms and suites, ten restaurants and bars, a 1,000 m² Valmont spa with adults-only indoor pool and nine treatment rooms.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_tables0: Three Michelin stars, 19/20 in the Gault&Millau. Arnaud Donckele signs a cuisine built on the fish of the Mediterranean, his jus, sauces and vinaigrettes now signatures. Terrace facing the baie des Canoubiers, beneath the pines — the gala dinner of the stay, to be booked far in advance.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_tables1: A family house born in 1912 as a fisherman's hut, a restaurant since 1953, in the secret cove of Bonne Terrasse. Wood-fired bouillabaisse (scorpionfish, gurnard, John Dory from the gulf), spiny lobster and the day's catch, feet in the sand. Boat shuttle available: the peninsula's authentic counterpoint.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_exp0: The Pampelonne institution since 1955, 43 boulevard Patch, a few hundred metres from Shellona. A lunch of wild fish and crudités under the tamarisks, mattresses, parasols and straw huts, pontoon and boat shuttle. Open daily from 10:00 to 17:00 — the ideal morning before a night at the beach club.
2026-07-18|Shellona St-Tropez — saison m.sej_exp1: A 170-hectare family estate perched on the hills of Gassin, one of the Côtes de Provence crus classés since 1955 and one of the last still harvesting by hand. Guided tour of the estate and the cellar followed by a tasting of three wines, with a panoramic view over the sea and the hilltop villages.

2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.n: L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — 2026 dinner show “Luxury Futuristic World”
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.dt: Season from 30 April to 4 October 2026, every evening (dinner & show from 19:30, DJ after midnight)
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.ds: Restaurant-cabaret facing the harbour, a jet-set address for more than ten years. Cuisine and artistic shows are reinvented each season under the direction of artist Philippe Shangti (2026 collection “Luxury Futuristic World”): cabaret, electro violinists, performances on the tables, then a dancing DJ set. Way in: table booking (dinner show) online or by telephone.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sw: “Luxury Fantasy” dinner show on the harbour: performers, fine dining and champagne until the very end of the night.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.ci: An institution on the port of Saint-Tropez, a see-and-be-seen dinner show frequented by celebrities
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.pe: Artistic direction by Philippe Shangti; international cabaret artists; celebrity and jet-set clientele
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.iv_w: Book online at opera-saint-tropez.com/fr/reservation/ or by telephone
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_pitch: Facing the yachts and the pink stone of the old town, L'Opéra stages a dinner show every evening from 19:30, where world cuisine with Mediterranean accents meets living tableaux inspired by Philippe Shangti's 2026 collection “Luxury Futuristic World” — then the DJ takes over the decks at midnight. All around, the peninsula unfolds its art de vivre: a palace hotel a step from the port, the only three-star table on the French Riviera, the legendary sand of Pampelonne and the Grand Cru Classé rosés from the hills of Gassin. Three days are enough to string it all together without ever driving more than twenty minutes.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_hotels0: An emblematic address since 1931, at the entrance to the village and a few minutes' walk from the Vieux Port — and therefore from L'Opéra. Interiors signed Sybille de Margerie, cantilevered pool and Les Toits rooftop, Spa by Clarins, La Serena private beach. 1 Traverse de la Gendarmerie, 83990 Saint-Tropez — +33 (0)4 83 09 60 00.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_hotels1: A village within the village, between the Citadelle and the Place des Lices: 86 rooms and suites in Mediterranean architecture, Byblos Spa by Sisley, Italian restaurant Il Giardino, rooftop Skybar — and Les Caves du Roy to carry the night on after the show. 20 avenue Paul Signac, 83990 Saint-Tropez — +33 (0)4 94 56 68 00.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_tables0: Three stars in the 2026 Guide MICHELIN, the only three-starred table on the French Riviera. Here Arnaud Donckele signs an obsessive reading of Mediterranean fish and of the sauces that have become his signature, right at the water's edge on la Bouillabaisse beach. Open every evening in season, except Wednesday.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_tables1: A MICHELIN-starred table perched on the ridges of Ramatuelle, facing the bay. Chef Éric Canino composes a luminous Provençal score — vegetables, fish, aromatic herbs and olive oil — running against the current of opulence, fifteen minutes from the port. 736 chemin des Crêtes, 83350 Ramatuelle.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_exp0: The Pampelonne institution since 1955, run by the de Colmont family under the motto “here the customer is not king… because he is a friend”. Lunch beneath the tamarisks, sun loungers facing the open sea, beach cabins, bar and boutique. 2026 season: 27 March to 1 November. 43 boulevard Patch, 83350 Ramatuelle — +33 (0)4 94 55 55 55.
2026-04-30|L'Opéra Saint-Tropez — dîner-.sej_exp1: One of the 23 estates distinguished as Grand Cru Classé of the Côtes de Provence in 1955, whose rosés rank among the best known in the world. Cellar visit, guided tasting and boutique on the estate, ten minutes from Saint-Tropez, at the heart of the peninsula's vineyards. Quartier Minuty, route de la Berle, 83580 Gassin.
