2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.iv_g: Lunch and evening by reservation (beach/beach club)
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.iv_w: Online booking via saintramatuelle.com (SevenRooms platform), tel +33 4 27 36 60 01, email info@saintramatuelle.com
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_pitch: Three days tuned to the rhythm of the peninsula: waking among hills of vines and umbrella pines, a barefoot day on the sand of Pampelonne, where SAINT — the Bagatelle Group's new address, open since 26 June 2026 — runs from sunrise pilates to a Mediterranean lunch and a sunset DJ set. Between two beaches, Arnaud Denckele's three-star table in Saint-Tropez and Éric Canino's kitchen garden on the heights of Ramatuelle give the stay its gastronomic backbone. The rest plays out in the shade of a Gassin cru classé and on the jetty of a 1955 beach institution: here, luxury is a question of slowness, not of display.
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_hotels0: A 19th-century palace hotel set in a 13-hectare park of umbrella pines, overlooking the bay of Pampelonne: 86 rooms and suites, spa, pools and a constellation of tables (Matsuhisa by Nobu, Palladio, Les Tables du Jardin). The decisive asset for this stay: its private beach, Jardin Tropezina, on the sand of Pampelonne, minutes from SAINT.
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_hotels1: The Saint-Tropez icon since 1967, a Provençal village of colourful façades steps from the place des Lices: 86 rooms and suites (several redesigned by Laura Gonzalez in 2025-2026), Byblos Spa by Sisley, Il Giardino and the legendary Caves du Roy for the after. Its Byblos Beach in Ramatuelle puts guests on the same sand as SAINT.
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_tables0: Three stars in the Guide MICHELIN 2026 and 19/20 at Gault&Millau: a cuisine of sauces, jus and broths built around the fish of the Mediterranean, served facing the bay of the Bouillabaisse. The peninsula's benchmark dinner — evening service only, booking essential several weeks ahead in high season.
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_tables1: Two MICHELIN stars in 2026, seven minutes from Pampelonne on the ridges of Ramatuelle: vegetables and herbs from the house kitchen garden, olive oil, rock fish, brief cooking and barely any fat — the Mediterranean at its most luminous. A due-south terrace above one of the finest bays on the coast, ideal for dinner after a day at SAINT.
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_exp0: A Provence cru classé and family estate since the 1930s, ten minutes from Pampelonne: guided visit of the cellar and the vines, then a tasting in the caveau (Prestige, 281, Gabriel, Or cuvées). The late-morning appointment before heading back down to the beach — 2491 route de la Berle, quartier Minuty, Gassin; book online.
2026-06-26|SAINT Ramatuelle — nouveau be.sej_exp1: The Pampelonne institution since 1955, a few hundred metres from SAINT: crudités served in a basket, grilled fish, tables under the tamarisks, boutique and cabanas, with mooring at the jetty for those arriving by boat. Open 27 March to 1 November 2026, lunch only — 43 boulevard Patch, Ramatuelle.

2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.dt: 2026 season estimated from 1 April to 31 October, every evening approx. 20:00–5:00 (dinner-cabaret, then club) — season dates to be reconfirmed with the venue
2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.ds: Nikkei restaurant (Japanese-Peruvian fusion) overlooking the village and the port, with live performers during dinner before turning into a club until dawn (DJs and shows). Intimate, sophisticated atmosphere, a true jet-set spot. Way in: table reservation via the online form or by telephone (entry approx. 30-50 €).
2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.sw: Dinner-cabaret, then club: performances, Nikkei gastronomy and a Tropezian night in the hushed setting of Gaïo.
2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.ci: The peninsula's fashionable dinner-club, a chic alternative to Caves du Roy / VIP Room
2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.pe: Resident and guest DJs plus cabaret and live artists (e.g. 2025 season: Mat Fellous, Milan Seguin); jet-set 'see and be seen' crowd — no confirmed global star on the bill
2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.iv_g: Dinner-cabaret + club, tables and bottles; access mainly through table reservation
2026-04-01|Gaïo Saint-Tropez — dîner-cab.iv_w: Table reservation via the 'Book a table' form on gaio.club, by telephone (+33 4 94 97 89 98) or email (headoffice@gaio.club)

2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.n: Le Bâoli Cannes — Summer Season 2026 (club & festive dinner)
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.dt: Summer season 2026, open in the evenings throughout the summer (indicative dates, recurring, undated)
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.ds: A jet-set institution of the Croisette at the far end of Port Canto, Le Bâoli moves from restaurant dinner to nightclub in a setting worthy of an Indonesian palace facing the Mediterranean (up to around 700 seated covers). Resident DJs and European guests, bottle tables and guest list. It is one of the historic spots through which the society and celebrity crowd of the Festival and of the Cannes summer parades.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sw: The institution of Cannes nightlife: dinner facing the yachts, then the club under the palm trees, celebrities in season.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.ci: The society address of Cannes par excellence, 'see and be seen' at the water's edge; pure Croisette/Riviera glamour DNA.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.pe: No named or confirmed international headliner. Resident DJs + European guests (e.g. Goraab). Recurring celebrity/jet-set clientele (an iconic Festival de Cannes spot), but a society crowd rather than a verifiable programmed artist. Access by table/dinner.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.iv_g: Table + minimum spend (bottles), or restaurant dinner; the club is accessible after dinner
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.iv_w: Table/dinner reservation through the official portal booking.baolicannes.com (Reservation button on baolicannes.com) — max 14 days in advance, up to 25 people; beyond that by email reservations@baolicannes.com or by telephone. The club cannot be booked separately (access after dinner).
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_pitch: The Cannes summer plays out at the eastern tip of the Croisette, where the yachts of Port Pierre Canto almost touch the umbrella pines. Here you take on the rhythm of the South: a siesta in the shade of an Art Deco palace hotel, lunch at the water's edge on an island reachable only by sea, then a late dinner at Le Bâoli before the night tips over into the club. A stay in which luxury is never demonstrative, only self-evident.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_hotels0: A legendary palace hotel dating from 1929, at 73 boulevard de la Croisette — a few minutes' walk from Port Canto. Art Deco façade, penthouses facing the bay, L'Oasis spa, private beach and the house's own starred restaurant. The closest address to Le Bâoli among the palace hotels of Cannes.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_hotels1: 58 boulevard de la Croisette. The 1913 icon, entirely reimagined by Tristan Auer and reopened in 2023: 332 rooms, suites and residences, a patio garden of 22,000 plants, the largest infinity pool in Cannes, a spa and Carlton Beach Club.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_tables0: One star in the Guide MICHELIN, Mediterranean cuisine signed by Jean Imbert: fish served raw or grilled over a wood fire, terrace plunging towards the Croisette. Special menus on summer fireworks evenings (22 July, 4, 15 and 24 August).
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_tables1: A Mediterranean table and a 1,000 m² private beach on the island of Sainte-Marguerite, reachable only by sea. Boat shuttle from the harbour master's office at Port Canto — the same quay as Le Bâoli. Cuisine by chef Yiannis Kioroglou, lunches from April to October, dinners on Thursdays, Fridays and Saturdays in summer. Reservation essential.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_exp0: Twenty minutes by boat from Cannes: a monastic island where twenty-one Cistercian monks have tended the vines for sixteen centuries. Tasting of the cuvées and liqueurs in the cellar, a walk between the rows facing the sea, sung offices in the church open to visitors. The perfect counterpoint to a night at Le Bâoli.
2026-07-18|Le Bâoli Cannes — saison esti.sej_exp1: The palace hotel's historic beach, a limited number of loungers facing the bay, lunch with your feet in the sand and a beach club in the afternoon. Booking a mattress is indispensable in high season.

2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.n: Black Coffee — Summer Residency at Shellona (2 Headline Nights)
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.dt: Upcoming headline date: Tuesday 4 August 2026 (the first date, 16 July 2026, passed on 17 July). Table reservation with an increased minimum spend on headline nights.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.ds: Two flagship nights from South African superstar Black Coffee (Grammy Award winner, emblematic resident of Ibiza and Mykonos) at the Shellona beach club on Pampelonne. Sunset, deep house and a champagne table: the archetype of the exceptional night out in the gulf of Saint-Tropez. Way in: a table with an increased minimum spend on headline nights (SevenRooms reservation).
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sw: The South African house legend in residency at Shellona: two exceptional nights on the sands of Pampelonne.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.ci: Global deep-house superstar; dates sold out several weeks in advance; UHNW crowd arriving by yacht and helicopter
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.iv_g: Table with minimum spend (increased on headline nights, ~1,000-5,000 € depending on artist and location)
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.iv_w: Table reservation via SevenRooms (official link from shellona.com); or telephone +33 4 94 43 87 34 / email pampelonne@shellonabeach.com
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_pitch: Two nights on the peninsula, set to Pampelonne's most beautiful hour: the one when the sand is still warm and Black Coffee takes over the decks at Shellona, chemin des Tamaris, on Tuesday 4 August 2026 — his second and final headline date of the summer, the first, on 16 July, having already passed. The stay is based in the hills of Ramatuelle, ten minutes from the beach: a nap under the pines, lunch with your feet in the sand, a Cru Classé rosé tasting at golden hour, then a haute-cuisine dinner before heading down to the sea. Table booked well in advance at Shellona — increased minimum spend on headline nights — and a valet arranged for the return, since the Tropezian art of living dictates that one never checks the time.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_hotels0: A contemporary palace hotel set on the Ramatuelle cliffside, chemin de la Quessine, about a 10-minute drive from Shellona and Pampelonne beach. Rooms and villas facing the open sea, a 1,000 m² Nescens spa occupying an entire floor (11 treatment rooms, a 12 m indoor pool with sea view, a 30 m outdoor pool, hammam), and two in-house restaurants including the two-Michelin-starred La Voile. The ideal base camp for recovering from a headline night.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_hotels1: An Italian-inspired villa nestled in a three-hectare pine grove above the bay of Pampelonne, 1100 chemin de Val de Rian in Ramatuelle. Forty-five rooms and suites, a lagoon pool carved into the rock, a Pure Altitude spa and a shuttle to Saint-Tropez. A more intimate, greener mood, a few minutes from chemin des Tamaris.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_tables0: The only three-Michelin-starred restaurant on the Côte d'Azur, signed by Arnaud Donckele, at the water's edge on Bouillabaisse beach, Saint-Tropez. Sauces and jus of anthology, peninsula fish worked species by species, 19/20 in the Gault&Millau. Seasonal opening and reservations to be requested far in advance: schedule it the evening before the Black Coffee date, so nothing feels rushed.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_tables1: A Ramatuelle institution since 1953, in the secluded cove of Bonne Terrasse at the southern tip of Pampelonne. Wood-fired bouillabaisse, grilled lobster, fish landed by the fishermen of Saint-Tropez; à la carte from 25 to 89 €, Camille tasting menu at 130 €. The peninsula's moment-of-truth lunch, ten minutes from Shellona.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_exp0: The legendary Pampelonne beach, open here since 1955, at 43 boulevard Patch in Ramatuelle: sunbed 39 €, parasol 19 €, cabana 59 €, beach service, pontoon and boat shuttle. Open daily from 10:00 to 17:00, 27 March to 1 November 2026. To be slotted in gently on Wednesday 5 August, after the Black Coffee night.
2026-08-04|Black Coffee — résidence esti.sej_exp1: A family estate since 1936, Cru Classé of the Côtes de Provence, perched on the hills of Gassin with a view over the Mediterranean: 2491 route de la Berle. A guided tour of the cellar and the vines followed by a tasting of three wines, by reservation only. Best done in the late afternoon, fifteen minutes from Pampelonne, before heading back down to the beach.

2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.n: Sass Café Monte-Carlo — the celebrity dinner-club (summer nights)
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.ds: A Monegasque institution since 1993, Sass is the intimate dinner-show where live musicians and then a DJ tip the meal into a dance floor until dawn. Hushed decor and a highly international crowd — the star address of avenue Princesse Grace. A table to be booked: one of the rare tangible ways into the Riviera's "private villa / celebrity" atmosphere.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sw: The celebrity dinner-club in Monaco: piano bar, champagne and high society — the confidential address for the great nights.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.ci: Monaco's benchmark jet-set address, a step away from Larvotto and the SBM palace hotels; "see and be seen" at its purest.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.pe: Celebrity clientele documented only anecdotally (no guaranteed appearance and no announced headliner on any given night); Sass is a jet-set dinner-club, not an event with a dated star line-up.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.iv_g: paid; book at least 24 hours ahead, far earlier for the summer
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.iv_w: Direct reservation by telephone (at least 24 hours ahead), via the official site sasscafe.com or through the SevenRooms platform; dinner from 20:30, then live musicians and DJ until around 03:00.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_pitch: On avenue Princesse Grace, the Monegasque summer runs in reverse: you dine late, dance on the tables at Sass' Café until first light, then slip back to a suite a few steps away, facing Larvotto. The day is repaired with warm seawater and essential oils, before setting out again in style for a starred table and two kilometres of grand-cru racks carved into the rock since 1874. A short, dense, perfectly choreographed stay where everything is done on foot or three minutes by car.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_hotels0: 40 avenue Princesse Grace, steps from Sass' Café: 4 hectares of gardens on the peninsula, a sand-bottomed lagoon, SPA Clarins & myBlend and uninterrupted sea views. The most obvious address for walking home after the night.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_hotels1: 22 avenue Princesse Grace, immediate neighbour of Sass' Café and the only hotel on the bay of Monte-Carlo with its own private beach, with indoor and outdoor pools and 24-hour service, two minutes from the Grimaldi Forum.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_tables0: At the Monte-Carlo Bay, 40 avenue Princesse Grace: the Creole-Mediterranean cooking of Marcel Ravin, the hotel's organic kitchen garden and fish sourced under the Mr Goodfish charter. Dinner Tuesday to Saturday, 19:30-21:30 — perfect before crossing the avenue to Sass'.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_tables1: Place du Casino: the monument of the Riviera since 1987, Mediterranean cuisine by Alain Ducasse and Emmanuel Pilon, pastry by Sandro Micheli. Dinner Tuesday to Saturday 19:30-21:15, jacket required all year round.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_exp0: 2 avenue de Monte-Carlo: 6,600 m² facing the Mediterranean, a 25 m seawater pool heated to 29°C and drawn from 37 metres down, sauna, hammam, bespoke protocols with therapists, a doctor and a nutritionist. The best antidote to a Monegasque summer night.
2026-07-18|Sass Café Monte-Carlo — dîner.sej_exp1: An institution of the bay since 1928: cabanas and a pontoon on the private beach, an Art Deco Olympic seawater pool heated to 28°C, paddleboarding and kayaking, four restaurants including Elsa. Open mid-April to October, 09:00-20:00 at the height of summer.

2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.n: Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetary Health — the Prince Albert II Foundation gala dinner
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.dt: The 2026 edition is expected in September (recurring) — official date NOT yet published as of 17 July 2026; late September is an estimate, to be confirmed on montecarlogala.org / fpa2.org
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.ds: The flagship charity gala dinner of the Prince Albert II of Monaco Foundation (benefiting planetary health and the Princess Charlene Foundation), traditionally held in September. Past editions have drawn a global star line-up — DiCaprio, Nicole Kidman, Madonna, Eva Longoria, Orlando Bloom — with dinner, award presentations and a concert. Access by table or donation through the Foundation. Please note: the 2026 date has not yet been officially announced (late September is an estimate, to be confirmed).
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sw: The Prince Albert II Foundation gala: global stars, a charity auction and princely society.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.ci: Monaco's most star-studded society gala, bringing together the princely family, patrons and Hollywood stars; charity ticketing is the genuine way in.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.pe: VERIFIED track record (past editions): Leonardo DiCaprio, Nicole Kidman, Madonna, Eva Longoria, Orlando Bloom, Robert Redford. The 2026 line-up has not yet been announced.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.iv_o: table / seat / charitable donation (gala ticketing through the Foundation)
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.iv_g: paid (seat, table or donation); by registration with the Prince Albert II Foundation
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.iv_w: Official route: contact the Prince Albert II of Monaco Foundation (FPA2) to purchase a seat/table or make a donation — email planetgala@fpa2.org or tel. +377 98 98 81 01. Follow the announcement of the 2026 date and ticketing on montecarlogala.org and fpa2.org.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_pitch: Three days on the Rock to live the Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetary Health as an insider: trunks set down on Place du Casino, black tie for dinner a few steps from the venue of the evening, and the following day given over to what the Principality does best — sea water, the vine and Mediterranean light. Morning at the Thermes Marins, pool drawn from 77 metres below the surface facing the open sea; a light, plant-led lunch signed by Yannick Alléno; then down into the Caves de l'Hôtel de Paris, 1,500 m² carved out in 1874 beneath the palace hotel, a glass of champagne in hand. Important caveat: as of 22 July 2026, the official date of the 2026 edition still has not been published on montecarlogala.org or fpa2.org — we are holding rooms and tables on a late-September window, adjustable the moment it is announced.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_hotels0: The founding palace hotel of 1864, on Place du Casino, steps from the Casino and the Opéra Garnier — in other words, within heel-walking distance of the gala venue. Princesse Grace and Prince Rainier III suites, direct access to the 6,600 m² spa and pool complex and to the rooftop Wellness Sky Club, private beach. Tel. +377 98 06 30 00.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_hotels1: A Belle Époque masterpiece with a glass dome designed by Gustave Eiffel, 200 metres from the Hôtel de Paris. 262 rooms and suites including 13 Diamond Suites, direct access to the Thermes Marins Monte-Carlo and to the Michelin-starred Pavyllon. The quieter of the two addresses, ideal for coming in late without meeting the crowd.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_tables0: Three Michelin stars, the first hotel restaurant in history to win all three macarons (1990). Mediterranean cooking by Alain Ducasse and Emmanuel Pilon, pastry by Sandro Micheli, adjoining cellar among the vastest in the world. Dinner Tuesday to Saturday (19:30–21:15), lunch Friday and Saturday. Elegant dress required, jacket obligatory at dinner all year round — to be booked far ahead during a gala week.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_tables1: One Michelin star. Counter open onto the kitchen, plant-focused menu, seafood and lightly sweetened desserts — the exact counterpoint to the spirit of the gala. Served on the terrace of the Mediterranean garden patio with sea view in fine weather. Open daily, 12:00–14:30 and 19:00–22:30: the right option for a pre-gala lunch or a light supper afterwards.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_exp0: 6,600 m² devoted to well-being and preventive health, connected to the Hôtel de Paris and the Hôtel Hermitage. Sea-water pool drawn from 77 m deep and 400 m offshore, cold sea-water bath, two hammams, jacuzzi, solarium, face and body treatments. Open 7 days a week from 8:00 to 20:00, 2 avenue de Monte-Carlo. Tel. +377 98 06 69 00. To be slotted in on the morning of the gala.
2026-09-25|Monte-Carlo Gala for Planetar.sej_exp1: The largest hotel cellars in the world: 1,500 m² carved out in 1874, around 350,000 bottles across 2 km of racks (Pétrus, Yquem, Lafite, Romanée-Conti, century-old vintages). Discovery Tour guided by a Chef Sommelier, glass of champagne included, €250 per person, maximum 10 people, subject to a dinner booked in one of the Resort's restaurants. Private hire possible for a cocktail or a post-gala dinner.

2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.n: Nammos Baja Sardinia — 2026 beach club season (new opening)
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.dt: 2026 season open since 29 May; beach 11:00–18:00, restaurant 12:30–01:00; recurring DJ nights July–September (note: headliner dates announced as the season unfolds)
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.ds: Nammos' first Sardinian outpost — the jet-set beach-club brand born at Psarou (Mykonos) and since rolled out to Dubai, Cannes and Limassol. A restaurant at the water's edge between granite boulders and the Tyrrhenian Sea, Mediterranean sharing plates, magnums of champagne, and a mood that drifts from sun-drenched lunch to festive late tables. The same see-and-be-seen DNA as Nammos Mykonos.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sw: The Mykonos legend lands in Sardinia: festive Greek dining, DJs and jet set — the most anticipated opening of the summer.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.ci: Costa Smeralda / Baja Sardinia, a perfect Riviera filter — the same universe as Nammos Mykonos and Cannes. Table and daybed reservations open, sea access and shuttle.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.pe: World-class DJ line-up announced for the 2026 Phi Beach/Nammos season: Black Coffee, Peggy Gou, Jamie Jones, Damian Lazarus, Claptone, Michael Bibi, Seth Troxler, Bob Sinclar, Kungs, Argy, Themba, among others. International superyacht and jet-set crowd.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.iv_w: Book via nammos.com/sardinia, or through Phi Beach's official email/telephone
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_pitch: Baja Sardinia is the Costa Smeralda stripped of Porto Cervo's protocol: wind-sculpted granite blocks, a sea that turns turquoise at the very first cove, and the La Maddalena archipelago resting on the horizon. Nammos settles in for the season beneath the rocks of Phi Beach — sun lounger and lunch at the water's edge until 18:00, then a dinner that slips gently into the night until 01:00. All around, a quarter of an hour is enough to go from the coast's only starred table to a Vermentino cellar lost in the Gallura maquis.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_hotels0: The first Italian address in the Destination by Hyatt portfolio, set on 15 hectares of fragrant gardens at Loc. Li Mucchi Bianchi, Baja Sardinia (07021 Arzachena) — a few minutes from Phi Beach. 75 suites and rooms nestled among the granite formations, private coves and a jetty, infinity pool facing the La Maddalena marine protected area, Pure Seven Spa around a central patio. Three restaurants: Capogiro, Spazio, Cone Club. Porto Cervo 10 minutes away, Olbia 35 km.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_hotels1: An intimate five-star on Cala dei Ginepri, 07020 Baja Sardinia — minutes from the village and from Phi Beach. 34 luminous rooms blending Sardinian and Middle Eastern influences, plus 5 villas for those who want complete discretion. Private beach on the cove, infinity pool opening onto the bay. Tel. +39 0789 974311. The right choice when you prefer the scale of a house to that of a resort.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_tables0: The only Michelin star in Porto Cervo and in the whole Costa Smeralda, confirmed in the 2026 Italy guide. Italo Bassi, forty years in the trade, presides over an open kitchen on the Promenade du Port, facing the old harbour (Via Aga Khan 1, 07021 Porto Cervo). Two tasting menus — the house classics or the new creations, in several lengths — or à la carte, with oysters, caviar and the day's catch served in their purest form. 2026 season: 23 April to 29 September. Reservations +39 340 120 9574 or info@confusion-restaurant.com. About 20 minutes from Baja Sardinia.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_tables1: Nobu Matsuhisa's Sardinian outpost, inside the legendary Costa Smeralda hotel designed by Jacques Couëlle (Porto Cervo, about 20 minutes from Baja Sardinia). Japanese precision with Peruvian accents — black cod with miso, tiradito — facing the bay of Cala di Volpe. The house also holds the open-air Barbecue beside one of the largest seawater pools in the Mediterranean, plus Beefbar and Le Grand for Sardinian and Italian cuisine. Book through the hotel concierge.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_exp0: A contemporary-architecture winery on the S.P. Porto Cervo–Arzachena, km 1, about 10 minutes from Baja Sardinia. Founded in 2000, the estate spans 50 hectares of vines across 7 zones of the Arzachena district: Vermentino and Cannonau to the fore, rounded out by passiti and a classic-method sparkling. The visit runs from a walk through the vines to grasp the Gallura terroir, to a tour of the cantina from harvest to bottling, a contemporary art exhibition, then a tasting of five wines with Sardinian breads, cheeses and charcuterie. By reservation (info@surrau.it, +39 0789 82933). Open late in summer: 10:00–22:00 in July, 10:00–23:00 in August.
2026-05-29|Nammos Baja Sardinia — saison.sej_exp1: Sardinia's first national park (1994): more than 60 islands, 180 km of coastline, over 20,000 protected hectares of land and sea, directly opposite Baja Sardinia. On the programme, depending on the wind: Budelli and its pink beach, Spargi, Santa Maria, Razzoli, Caprera and Santo Stefano. Departures by gozzo or private yacht from Baja Sardinia, Cannigione or Palau, 15–25 minutes from the hotels. Anchorages and landings are regulated by the Ente Parco (Via Giulio Cesare 7, La Maddalena, +39 0789 790211): the itinerary is set the day before according to the weather and the permits in force.

2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.n: Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda — 2026 season & signature nights (Cala Petra Ruja)
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.dt: Season opens 29 May 2026; signature nights dated through 12 Sept.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.ds: Nikki Beach beach club in the bay of Cala Petra Ruja (Porto Cervo, Sardinia), reachable only by sea (private yacht or complimentary shuttle from the bay of Cala di Volpe). Season opens 29 May 2026. Signature 2026 programme (official source): "Dolce Sound" (3, 17, 31 July; 7, 21 Aug.; 4 Sept.), "A Tavola!" chef's table (6 Aug., 11 Sept.), "Felicità" Ferragosto (15 Aug.), "Ciao Disco!" every Saturday, "La Domenica Italiconica" every Sunday, closing party "Arrivederci Estate" on 12 Sept. New Cocktail Club opened 11 June on the Porto Cervo Waterfront. No named headliner announced.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sw: The Nikki spirit on the Costa Smeralda: white parties, champagne brunches and DJ sets facing the yachts of Porto Cervo.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.ci: The quintessential Riviera brand, from Saint-Tropez to Ibiza and Miami; here on the Costa Smeralda with sea access, daybeds and magnums — unapologetic jet-set glamour.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.pe: No named headliner announced; pure Nikki Beach DNA (Saint-Tropez, Ibiza, Miami), a yacht and VIP crowd.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.iv_g: Booking recommended; complimentary shuttle from the bay of Cala di Volpe (Cala di Volpe → Cala Petra Ruja, every 15 min, 11:00–18:00)
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.iv_w: Book via SevenRooms from nikkibeach.com/costa-smeralda, or by email/telephone
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_pitch: Between Porto Cervo and the cove of Cala Petra Ruja, the Costa Smeralda is not somewhere you visit: it is somewhere you live, to the rhythm of the sun. You arrive at Nikki Beach by sea — the complimentary shuttle leaves the bay of Cala di Volpe every fifteen minutes, from 11:00 to 18:00 — for a lunch that drifts into champagne and music, from July's "Dolce Sound" nights to the final "Arrivederci Estate" on 12 September, carried on into the evening at the Nikki Beach Cocktail Club on the harbour. The rest of the stay comes down to three gestures: a starred table above the old port, a Gallura winery at sunset, and the sails of the maxi-yachts gliding past the terrace.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_hotels0: The 1963 icon conceived as a fishing village by Jacques Couëlle: 121 unique rooms and suites, hand-made frescoes and ceramics, one of the largest seawater pools in the Mediterranean and Italy's first Shiseido Spa. A strategic address: it is from the bay of Cala di Volpe that the complimentary sea shuttle departs for Nikki Beach, at Cala Petra Ruja.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_hotels1: The white vessel of 1964, now under Belmond: arched rooms and rooftop terraces, private villas with pools, two pools and a long stretch of fine sand (Via Romazzino 4, Arzachena). Sardinian cuisine at La Terrasse, cooking classes, paddleboarding and the Pevero trail. 2026 season from 28 May to October, minutes from Cala Petra Ruja.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_tables0: The only Michelin star in Porto Cervo and on the Costa Smeralda (Italy Guide 2026). Via Aga Khan 1, in the heart of the Promenade du Port, above the old harbour: open kitchen, mirrors and gold, two tasting menus (the classics or the creations) and an à la carte list of noble ingredients — oysters, caviar, prime seafood. Bubble Bar & Bistrot on the level below for aperitivo. 2026 season: 23 April to 29 September; reservations +39 340 1209574.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_tables1: The star of Salvatore Camedda, a chef born in Cabras, at Piazzetta Due Vele in Baja Sardinia, facing the bay: contemporary Sardinian cooking built on the island's great products — bottarga from Cabras, casizolu, fiore sardo, wild herbs of the Costa Smeralda — with deliberately unexpected pairings. About twenty minutes from Porto Cervo, the perfect alternative to dinner on the harbour.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_exp0: From 6 to 12 September 2026, the world's greatest maxi-yachts contest the bay off Porto Cervo, under the aegis of the YCCS, founded in 1967 by the Aga Khan. The timing could not be better: the final race coincides with the "Arrivederci Estate" closing party at Nikki Beach on 12 September, and the Rolex Swan Cup follows from 13 to 19 September.
2026-05-29|Nikki Beach Costa Smeralda —.sej_exp1: Ten minutes from the beaches, a winery of contemporary architecture (local stone and glass façades) set among 50 hectares of Gallura vineyards. Guided experiences by reservation, from €45 to €90 per person: "The Excellences" with a cellar tour, "The colours of the terroir", "La Dolce Vita" or simply a gourmet aperitivo. Open until 22:00 from May to September, 23:00 in August — the perfect counterpoint to a day on the beach.

2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.n: Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — 2026 season (dinners & international DJ sets)
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.dt: Summer 2026 season, recurring — no event dates and no DJ programming published in advance (honest caveat: festive nights follow no calendar).
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.ds: Festive beach club from the Verde galaxy ("The World's Elite Festive Beach Club"), tucked into a cove of the Costa Smeralda (Poltu Quatu, Arzachena) reached by boat or sea shuttle, 10 minutes from Porto Cervo. Riviera-inspired Mediterranean cuisine under executive chef Yannick Plassart; festive evenings with music and DJ sets as the day fades. Open since July 2025, running through the 2026 season.
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.sw: Verde by Yeeels in the Poltu Quatu fjord: festive dinners and DJ sets in the most secret cove of the Costa.
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.ci: Poltu Quatu, a luxury borgo in full 2026 renaissance (the new W Sardinia); Verde is a high-end festive beach-club brand in the Riviera spirit.
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.pe: No named headliner published and no dated DJ line-up; positioning as an "elite festive beach club", with the musical programming never announced in advance.
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.iv_o: Table / sunbed booking (open to the public, by reservation)
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.iv_g: Reservation compulsory by telephone/WhatsApp; access to the cove by sea shuttle from the Poltu Quatu marina or by private yacht
2026-06-01|Verde Beach Poltu Quatu — sai.iv_w: Reservations only by telephone/WhatsApp or email via the official site verde-sardinia.com

2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.n: Notti Toscane 2026 — Andrea Bocelli gala (Alpemare & Villa Alpebella)
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.ds: Two charity gala nights hosted by Andrea Bocelli and the ABF Foundation. Night one: sunset aperitivo followed by a beachside dinner with live music at Alpemare Beach Club (beach cocktail dress code). Night two: gourmet dinner and gala concert at the Maestro's private villa, with international guest artists (gala attire). Genuine but selective access: participation is secured through a charitable contribution to the Andrea Bocelli Foundation (via Celebrity Adventures), not through open ticket sales.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sw: Andrea Bocelli sings the Versilia: a gala beneath the stars between Alpemare and Villa Bertelli — Tuscany in full evening dress.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.ci: The Bocelli family's beach club in Forte dei Marmi, the epicentre of Versilian glamour all summer long.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.pe: Andrea Bocelli and international guest artists (full line-up not published)
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.iv_o: By invitation through a charitable donation
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.iv_g: Contribution/patronage to the Andrea Bocelli Foundation via official organiser Celebrity Adventures (partner of the Bocelli family since 2014); the invitation is issued to donors. Note: ticket resale by third parties is flagged as fraudulent by the Foundation — use the official channel only.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.iv_w: Contact official organiser Sean Currie (Celebrity Adventures) to become a supporter of the Andrea Bocelli Foundation and receive the invitation — not available through open ticket sales.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_pitch: Two late-July nights when the Versilia plays its rarest score: on the 26th, a gala dinner on the sand at Bagno Alpemare, the Bocelli family's beach club, aperitivo at sunset then music facing the Tyrrhenian; on the 27th, the gates of Villa Alpebella open onto the Maestro's private garden for a gourmet dinner and a recital a few metres from the sea. In between, you live Forte dei Marmi like a regular: a cabana in the shade of the pines in the morning, Carrara marble and the Apuan Alps in the afternoon, fish suppers at night, all just a few hundred metres from the two gala venues. A three-night stay (25–28 July), chauffeured car for the evening transfers, white linen de rigueur.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_hotels0: A palace hotel and member of The Leading Hotels of the World, Viale Ammiraglio Morin 67 — the same seafront avenue as Bagno Alpemare (Viale Italico 69), a few minutes' walk from the gala on the 26th. Suites in signature Italian design, Egoista Spa, the private Dalmazia beach, the 67 Sky Lounge rooftop looking out on the Apuan Alps and the Michelin-starred Lux Lucis by chef Valentino Cassanelli. The ideal base for walking home after dinner on the sand. Tel. +39 0584 783636.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_hotels1: The historic five-star address of Forte dei Marmi, Viale Morin 169: a private park of umbrella pines, several villas including Villa Agnelli, two heated pools (one seafront with jacuzzi), day spa, fitness, two restaurants and a boat service. Its signature: the underground tunnel linking the park straight to the private Augustus Lido beach, without ever crossing the road. Tel. +39 0584 787200.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_tables0: The benchmark fine-dining table of Forte dei Marmi, run by the Vaiani family and starred in the Michelin guide: Versilian seafood cooking poised “between authentic flavours and experimentation”, in an intimate dining room with impeccable service. Book it for dinner on the evening of the 25th, to open the stay before the two gala nights. Reservations via the Contatti page of the website.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_tables1: A seafood institution in Forte dei Marmi since 1981, Via Giosuè Carducci 61, right in the centre a step from the market. The pescato del giorno presented at the table, a wine list saluted by Le Soste, Gambero Rosso, L'Espresso and Bibenda, and the ritual of the late lunch so dear to Forte dei Marmi regulars. Perfect for lunch on the 27th, before the evening at Villa Alpebella.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_exp0: The Bocelli family's beach club, Viale Italico 69, voted Best Beach Club Italy 2024 and 2025: 120 luxury cabanas facing the Tyrrhenian, the Alpemare and Marecalmo restaurants, bar and aperitivo. Enjoy it by day, sun lounger and lunch with your feet in the sand, on the very site of the gala dinner on the evening of 26 July. Beach reservations online, tel. +39 0584 1811042 / info@alpemare.com.
2026-07-26|Notti Toscane 2026 — gala And.sej_exp1: An excursion into the heart of the Apuan Alps quarries, departing from Colonnata (Strada Comunale per Colonnata 1), about 30 minutes from Forte dei Marmi. The Gold formula in a private vehicle with a dedicated guide, or bespoke Diamond for a special occasion: you come into direct contact with statuary marble, Michelangelo's own, before heading back down to lunch on lardo di Colonnata. Reservation required, tel. +39 0585 625112 / info@cavedimarmotours.com.

2026-08-06|Ravello Festival 2026 — Serat.n: Ravello Festival 2026 — Jazz Nights on the Belvedere of Villa Rufolo
2026-08-06|Ravello Festival 2026 — Serat.ds: On the legendary Belvedere suspended above the Amalfi Coast, three jazz nights from the 74th edition: Andrea Motis Trio (6th), Rita Marcotulli Ensemble (7th) and “Omaggio a Miles” with saxophonist Rick Margitza — who played alongside Miles Davis — Jeff Ballard, Dario Deidda… (8th). A glamorous bucket-list setting, sunset over infinity. Real access: official ticketing (from 40 €) online and box office on Piazza Duomo. (NB: other concerts from the same edition are already on the radar; these are the jazz nights, a separate strand.)
2026-08-06|Ravello Festival 2026 — Serat.sw: Jazz on the Belvedere of Villa Rufolo, 350 m above the sea: the concert staged in the most beautiful drawing room of the Mediterranean.
2026-08-06|Ravello Festival 2026 — Serat.ci: The Belvedere of Villa Rufolo, one of the most spectacular open-air stages in Europe and the summer rendezvous of the Amalfi Coast.
2026-08-06|Ravello Festival 2026 — Serat.pe: Rick Margitza (formerly with Miles Davis), Jeff Ballard, Andrea Motis, Rita Marcotulli, Dario Deidda
2026-08-06|Ravello Festival 2026 — Serat.iv_w: Purchase online on the official Fondazione Ravello website (fondazioneravello.com) or at the box office on Piazza Duomo, Ravello

2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.n: Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — masked gala ball of the Venice Carnival
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.dt: Saturday 6 February 2027 (official date announced on ilballodeldoge.com). The theme of the 2027 edition has not yet been revealed; the venue is announced by the authorised ticket office (Scuola Grande della Misericordia), the official website does not yet name it.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.ds: The costumed masked ball created by Antonia Sautter, 34th edition, reputed to be one of the most exclusive events in the world: gala dinner, dancing and performances (opera singers, musicians, burlesque artists, Commedia dell'Arte characters), after-party. Around 400 costumed guests. Period costume (15th-19th c.) strictly compulsory — a plain dinner jacket or day dress will be turned away — available to hire from the Antonia Sautter atelier (more than 1,500 costumes, fittings and accessories included in the Premium/Full categories). Named tickets, from €800 (After Dinner, entry at 00:00) and €1,600 (Doge's Lounge, entry at 23:00) up to €4,000 (section B), €6,000-7,000 (Supreme) and €10,000 (Doge's Imperial Table). Several sections are already sold out for 2027.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sw: Venice's most closed-door masked ball: four hundred guests in period costume, gala dinner, dancing and performances until dawn — you enter only in eighteenth-century dress, and never by chance.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.ci: The jet-set gala of the Venice Carnival, regularly ranked among the most exclusive parties on the planet, a sumptuous 'see and be seen' spirit in period costume.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.pe: Created by Antonia Sautter. Artistic programme: opera singers, musicians, burlesque artists, dancers and Commedia dell'Arte characters — to date no artist has been named and no international celebrity announced for 2027. An international, costumed high-society crowd.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.iv_g: Ticket categories from €800 (After Dinner) to €10,000 (Doge's Imperial Table); the Premium/Full categories include costume hire at the Antonia Sautter atelier (fittings, accessories)
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.iv_w: Purchase of named tickets (full name of each guest required) online on the official site ilballodeldoge.com, via the authorised ticket office veneziaopera-tickets.eu (event page with purchase button), or by email/telephone through the organiser's office. Period costume compulsory. 50% refund until 28 December 2026, none thereafter (reseller terms).
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_pitch: On Saturday 6 February 2027, Venice returns to its most beautiful lie: the hidden face. We set your trunks down in Cannaregio, a few bridges from the Scuola Grande della Misericordia, between a fifteenth-century palace opening onto the Grand Canal and two starred tables where the lagoon is eaten by the spoonful. A mask shaped by your own hands, a private motoscafo to the threshold of the ball, the return at first light through an empty city: all that is left is to step into the role.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_hotels0: The closest to the ball: a fifteenth-century palace listed as a national monument, Campo Santa Sofia 4198/99 (Ca' d'Oro), Cannaregio — only a few bridges from the Scuola Grande della Misericordia, the venue announced by the ticket office. 42 rooms and suites, a monumental marble staircase, a frescoed ballroom, the L'Alcova restaurant and a panoramic terrace over the Grand Canal, facing the Rialto market. The house where you dress in costume in your own suite and walk there.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_hotels1: Calle Tiepolo 1364, San Polo: one of the eight monumental palaces on the Grand Canal, with private gardens — an absolute rarity in Venice. The Arva restaurant, The Bar and the Palazzo Kitchen Table for a dinner behind closed doors. Spa on the third floor (thermal waters from Salsomaggiore Terme, three treatment rooms, rooftop fitness studio). Arrival by boat from Marco Polo, around 50 minutes; departure by private motoscafo to Cannaregio on the evening of the ball.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_tables0: Two Michelin stars, Calle Tron 1961, Santa Croce. Enrico Bartolini signs the menu, resident chef Donato Ascani stages it: a contemporary reading of lagoon cooking, served in a palace opening onto a garden on the Grand Canal. The dinner of the evening before, the one that sets the tone for the weekend.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_tables1: One Michelin star, Piazza San Marco 121, on the noble floors of the Gran Caffè Quadri, opened in 1775 — the former rooms of the Venetian officers, full-frame windows onto the square. Chef Sergio Preziosa, a rereading of Italian and Venetian classics. The lunch of the day after, masks put away, while the Carnival parades beneath the windows.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_exp0: Dorsoduro 3215: a family atelier that has been shaping Venetian papier-mâché masks entirely by hand since 1984. Small-group making and decorating classes — you leave with the mask you will wear to the ball, moulded and painted by your own hands. The only way never to meet anyone else behind the same face.
2027-02-06|Il Ballo del Doge 2027 — bal.sej_exp1: Fondamenta S. Caterina 3, on the island of Mazzorbo, a step from Burano: the walled vineyard of Dorona di Venezia, the lagoon's native grape, with its cellar, its starred table and its Osteria Contemporanea. A lagoon escape by private boat, a tasting among the vines, lunch facing the water. Seasonal opening to be confirmed for February — we lock the date in at the time of booking.

2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.dt: October 2026 — exact date, venue and prices not yet released by the organisers (the 2025 edition was held on 8 November)
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.ds: Philanthropic gala founded by Maria Bravo, the Paris stop on an international circuit (Cannes, Marbella, London). Gala dinner, live performances and a charity auction bringing together celebrities and international patrons, red carpet. Tickets and tables bookable through the official office.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sw: Red carpet, gala dinner and auctions under the spotlights: the Paris stop on the Global Gift circuit, where the Marbella and Cannes jet set comes to shine for a good cause.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.ci: Global Gift jet-set gala circuit; dinner + show + charity auction format in a Paris palace hotel.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.pe: 2026 line-up not yet announced. Previous editions (2025, press-verified): Eva Longoria, Maria Bravo, performances by Vitaa and M. Pokora, appearances by Kendji Girac, Slimane and Vincent Niclo.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.iv_g: ticketing not yet open for Paris 2026; access by RSVP request (individual seats and tables) through the official office
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.iv_w: Official page still reads “Coming Soon”: contact the Global Gift Foundation office by e-mail or telephone as published on globalgiftgala.com to request a seat or a table; watch for ticketing to open at globalgiftgala.com/events/ (the other 2026 stops are already priced there: Marbella €1,000–5,000, Monaco €1,500–5,000).
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_pitch: In October 2026, the Golden Triangle dresses up for philanthropy. As the 2025 Global Gift Gala was held at the Four Seasons Hôtel George V, we anchor the stay on that exact point: everything is done on foot, from the palace hotels of avenue Montaigne to the most starred tables of the Right Bank, without ever getting back into a car. Since the exact date, venue and prices have not yet been published by the organiser, we hold flexible options and lock everything in the moment the official announcement lands.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_hotels0: 31 avenue George V, 75008 — The palace hotel that hosted the 2025 edition of the gala. Staying here means taking the lift down to the party: flower-filled courtyards by Jeff Leatham, suites with rooftop terraces, spa and overnight service. The safest address should the 2026 edition return to the same venue.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_hotels1: 42 avenue Gabriel, 75008 — Five-star palace hotel on the service lane of the Champs-Élysées, halfway between Faubourg Saint-Honoré and avenue Montaigne. Private-mansion spirit signed Jacques Garcia, just 40 rooms and suites, views of the Grand Palais and the Eiffel Tower: the discreet alternative, some ten minutes on foot from avenue George V.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_tables0: 15 rue Lamennais, 75008 — Two Michelin stars, seven minutes on foot from avenue George V. An institution of Parisian classicism, carried by a cellar of more than 3,800 wines and spirits and food-and-wine pairings of rare precision. The dinner the night before — the one where the real conversations happen.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_tables1: 6 rue Balzac, 75008 — Chef Pierre Gagnaire's restaurant, a few streets from the George V. Signature cuisine built as series of small plates that answer one another: the address to book when you want an evening that surprises rather than one that reassures. Reserve very far ahead.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_exp0: 37 avenue Hoche, 75008 — A beauty ritual on the afternoon of the gala: one of the largest palace-hotel spas in Paris, with a swimming pool and bespoke treatment rooms. You leave rested, fifteen minutes from the evening's venue.
2026-10-01|The Global Gift Gala Paris 20.sej_exp1: Yachts and private salons on the Seine, including the Don Juan II, where chef Frédéric Anton presides. The day after the gala: lunch or dinner at the quayside, then a cruise, illuminated monuments on either bank, a table set for your guests alone. The most elegant way to extend the evening without repeating it.

2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.dt: Thu 19 November 2026, from 19:30 — Fondation Chaillot gala dinner (3rd edition). The 2026 artistic programme has not yet been announced; online bookings are already open.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.ds: Third edition of the Fondation Chaillot gala evening, held inside the Palais de Chaillot: a patronage evening bringing together donors, artists and partners in the Foyer de la Danse, an Art Deco room facing the Eiffel Tower head-on. All funds raised go in full to the Fondation Chaillot (under the aegis of the Fondation de France, chaired by Laurent Martinet) to support choreographic creation, the touring of works, digital projects and access to culture. Previous editions paired an exclusive performance with a haute gastronomie dinner and a DJ set; the programme for 19 November 2026 has not yet been published. Seats and tables can be booked online right now.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sw: Beneath the Art Deco gilding of the Foyer de la Danse, facing an Eiffel Tower placed there like a private stage set: haute cuisine, dance at its finest and a DJ set until the small hours.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.ci: Prestige foundation gala at the Palais de Chaillot; Art Deco Foyer de la Danse with an unobstructed view of the Eiffel Tower; long-standing patrons Dior Couture and L'Oréal; an audience of donors, collectors and business leaders.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.pe: The 2026 artistic programme has not been announced to date. Past record (not to be attributed to 2026): 2025 edition — an excerpt from TORDRE by Rachid Ouramdane, dinner by Yannick Alléno, DJ set by Barbara Butch; 2024 edition — Contre-Nature by Rachid Ouramdane, an excerpt by Sharon Eyal, menu by Tatiana Levha, desserts by Cédric Grolet.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.iv_o: Fondation Chaillot (under the aegis of the Fondation de France) / Chaillot – Théâtre national de la Danse
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.iv_g: Rates published on the official site: individual seat €1,000 (Donateur) or €2,000 (Mécène, premium placement in the Foyer de la Danse); table of 10 €10,000 or €20,000 (premium placement). Tax deduction of 66% on income tax (IR), 75% on the real-estate wealth tax (IFI, capped at €50,000), 60% on corporate tax (IS).
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.iv_w: Online booking is open via the « JE RESERVE » button on the official site, which leads to the dedicated Fondation de France donation page: https://dons.fondationdefrance.org/Chaillot2026/~mon-don (options: buy a Donateur seat, a Mécène seat or a table for 10; for several seats, use the free-amount field). International gateways published: donorbox.org (USA) and hk.givergy.com (Asia). For a €20,000 table, write directly to the patronage department.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_pitch: The gala takes place in the Foyer de la Danse, beneath the gilding of the Palais de Chaillot, facing the Eiffel Tower. All around, the 16th arrondissement unfolds its own art de vivre: a palace hotel set in the former private mansion of Prince Roland Bonaparte, five minutes away on foot; signature tables from the Bois de Boulogne to the rue de Longchamp; and the Seine, which you sail up by electric boat from the Port Debilly, at the foot of the Trocadéro. Two nights are enough to turn a patronage evening into a collector's stay.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_hotels0: Palace hotel in the 16th arrondissement, set in the former private mansion of Prince Roland Bonaparte, a listed Historic Monument, facing the Eiffel Tower and a few minutes' walk from the Trocadéro. 100 rooms and suites, Chi The Spa, the Cantonese restaurant Shang Palace, Maison Roland and Les Salons du Prince. The obvious base for a gala dinner at Chaillot.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_hotels1: A contemporary counterpoint in the 16th arrondissement: 7,000 m² designed by Philippe Starck inside a former 1970s postal sorting centre, 59 rooms and 6 suites. A 22-metre pool, sauna, hammam, salt cave, Clarins treatment rooms and a rooftop above the roofs of Paris — ideal for recovering the day after the gala.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_tables0: In spirit, the table closest to the Trocadéro: Pascal Barbot in the kitchen, Christophe Rohat in the dining room, in the 16th arrondissement. Often wild produce, chosen one by one, cooking driven by instinct rather than display. The Salon Joël can be booked privately for 6 to 14 guests for an intimate pre-gala lunch.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_tables1: Three Michelin stars in the heart of the Bois de Boulogne, with Frédéric Anton at the helm since 1997, under the Maison Lenôtre banner. A Second Empire pavilion among the trees, ten minutes by car from the Palais de Chaillot: lunch the day after, or dinner the evening before.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_exp0: Boarding at the Port Debilly, literally at the foot of the Trocadéro: a silent, 100% electrically powered boat where you dine on a menu signed by Alain Ducasse and on-board chef Jean-Philippe Berens, with around a hundred wines in the cellar. Lunch or dinner; Paris glides past behind the wide picture windows.
2026-11-19|3e soirée de gala de la Fonda.sej_exp1: The legendary 1929 pool, brought back to life in the 16th arrondissement: winter and summer basins, La Plage by Clarins on the terrace, and one of the largest hotel spas in Europe (1,600 m², 13 treatment rooms, hammams, Hydrafacial, Icoone). A 1,500 m² Club Molitor and a panoramic rooftop to end the stay above the rooftops.
